raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves strengthening or reframing the thesis, contribution, and benchmark motivation above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the argument from benchmark saturation to the need for RigorDTI follows coherently.","R1.3":"Because the draft relies on reported benchmark ranges and dispersion claims, improvement requires aligning claims with the stated empirical evidence.","R1.5":"The draft may need section-level and narrative reordering to present motivation, benchmark construction, results, and implications more effectively.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the plain request to improve a draft strongly entails improving clarity, flow, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about unstated data, methods, and literature beyond the artifact.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong phrases such as definitive, transformative, and decisively resolves, so avoiding overclaim is central.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibility and not actually revise or critique the draft.","RX.4":"Improvement responsibilities should avoid encouraging misleading benchmark authority or unsupported community replacement claims.","RX.5":"The projection should track which responsibilities arise from the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported external assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves strengthening or reframing the thesis, contribution, and benchmark motivation above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the argument from benchmark saturation to the need for RigorDTI follows coherently.
rationale.R1.3: Because the draft relies on reported benchmark ranges and dispersion claims, improvement requires aligning claims with the stated empirical evidence.
rationale.R1.5: The draft may need section-level and narrative reordering to present motivation, benchmark construction, results, and implications more effectively.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the plain request to improve a draft strongly entails improving clarity, flow, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about unstated data, methods, and literature beyond the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong phrases such as definitive, transformative, and decisively resolves, so avoiding overclaim is central.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibility and not actually revise or critique the draft.
rationale.RX.4: Improvement responsibilities should avoid encouraging misleading benchmark authority or unsupported community replacement claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should track which responsibilities arise from the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported external assumptions.
